description_long: This object consists of two firearms with long barrels and revolver-style cylinders, featuring metallic bodies and wooden grips. The barrels are elongated and dark, with a matte finish, while the frames are a lighter metallic color, possibly brass or steel. The wooden grips are ornately carved with visible patterns, adding a decorative touch. The cylinders are exposed and appear to be removable, as seen in the disassembled views. The trigger guards are rounded and prominent, providing protection for the trigger area. The overall design suggests a hybrid between a revolver and a shotgun, with a focus on both functionality and aesthetic detail.
description: A pair of long-barreled firearms with metallic frames, wooden carved grips, and exposed revolver-style cylinders, blending revolver and shotgun features.
description_view_0: Two firearms are shown side by side, highlighting the long barrels, metallic frames, and detailed wooden grips.
description_view_1: The firearms are partially disassembled, revealing the removable cylinders and internal components.
description_view_2: A top-down angle emphasizes the length of the barrels and the ornate carvings on the wooden grips.
description_view_3: The firearms are shown with their cylinders and barrels separated, providing a clear view of the trigger guards and internal mechanisms.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single long barrel per firearm, no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with smooth edges and no finger grooves
